steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: Left side: “This is why many ranching terms come from Spanish” is an independent clause. Right side: “The English word “lasso” (a rope) comes from the Spanish word lazo, and “cinch” (a belt) comes from cincho” is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for separating independent clauses
steps.content:
The rule for separating two independent clauses is to use a period.

Option A uses a period, correctly applying this rule. B, C, and
  • D violate the rule: B uses parentheses (inappropriate for this boundary)
  • C has no punctuation (creating a run-on)
  • D applies a question mark (wrong for a non-interrogative clause)
.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
